pass"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-11-11T18:28:23Z","updated_at":"2024-11-14T12:54:27Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Minimal Reproducible Example

```fortran
program main
    implicit none
    
    type :: Circle
        real :: radius = 1.0
    end type Circle
    
    call print_radius(Circle(1.5), Circle())

contains

    subroutine print_radius(m, n)
        type(Circle), intent(in) :: m, n
        print *, m%radius, n%radius
    end subroutine

end program main
```

### Output

#### GFortran

```console
(base) saurabh-kumar@Awadh:~/Projects/System/lfortran$ gfortran ./examples/example.f90 && ./a.out
   1.50000000       1.00000000 
```

#### LFortran

```console
(base) saurabh-kumar@Awadh:~/Projects/System/lfortran$ lfortran ./examples/example.f90
(base) saurabh-kumar@Awadh:~/Projects/System/lfortran$ 
``` 

This happens because the subroutine call is removed in the `class_constructor` pass. It occurs only for cases when the class instances passed directly to the subroutine call are more than one; for a single argument (`fn(cls())`), the program executes correctly.

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5414","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5414/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5414/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5414/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5414","id":2670351573,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6fKlTV","number":5414,"title":"Speeding up time to compile LFortran","user":{"login":"certik","id":20568,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNTY4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20568?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik","html_url":"https://github.com/certik","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2024-11-19T00:00:07Z","updated_at":"2024-11-19T13:47:30Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Let's speedup the compilation of the C++ source code of LFortran. We identified at least the following potential speedups:

* [ ] split `asr.h` into smaller parts, and only use what we need; the stock `asr.h` should only contain the ASR definitions that are needed in all of the code base, but specialied visitors, possibly all visitors should be separate
* [ ] modify cmake to not build a library of libasr, but just collect all the files and build everything at once (currently the parallel compilation stalls waiting for the library to be created and linked --- single point bottleneck for some reason in cmake)
* [ ] get rid of some binaries that we have (cpptranslate, various parsing benchmarks), or not build them by default --- it seems link time is very slow.
* [ ] optimize our include header files, and try not to include 3rd party libs at all and minimize C++ stdlib includes (which can also be slow). Only include 3rd party stuff in .cpp files where the damage is limited.
